mapping.a_element: dispersionless limit of the Hirota equations for KP and Toda hierarchies
tau-function of dispersionless KP hierarchy providing solutions to associativity (WDVV) equations
mapping.b_element: solutions to the dKP equation embedded in Einstein-Weyl (EW) geometry

mapping.a_element: associativity (WDVV) equations as a governing structure
mapping.b_element: dKP equation as a governing structure within EW geometry

mapping.a_element: infinite-dimensional solution space of the dispersionless hierarchy
mapping.b_element: solutions to dKP equation parameterized by arbitrary functions (e.g., one function of one variable)

mapping.a_element: Lax representation or integrability structure of the hierarchy
mapping.b_element: Lax representation of the EW condition and its relation to hyper-Hermitian metrics

brief_justification: Both papers center on the dispersionless Kadomtsev-Petviashvili (dKP) equation as a core integrable structure: Paper A derives the associativity (WDVV) equations from the dispersionless limit of KP/Toda hierarchies, while Paper B embeds the dKP equation within Einstein-Weyl geometry and uses its integrability (e.g., Lax representation) to construct solutions. The shared machinery is the dKP equation and its role as a governing integrable system with infinite-dimensional solution spaces.
